description: One of the earliest crime families in New York City, based in Italian Harlem. Founded by Giuseppe Morello in the 1890s, it dominated the Italian underworld by defeating the Brooklyn Camorra. The family engaged in racketeering, counterfeiting, and murder, and was the predecessor of the Genovese crime family. It was active until 1931.

country_links.evidence_quote: recovered a large amount of American and Canadian counterfeit bills.
country_links.context: The Morellos possessed Canadian counterfeit bills recovered in a police raid.

country_links.evidence_quote: printing $5 bills in Sicily and smuggling them into the United States.
country_links.context: The Morello family printed counterfeit $5 bills in Sicily and smuggled them into the United States.
